name: Filter Drawer
description: A side drawer containing categorized filter options with checkboxes. Opens from the right side of the screen, allowing users to select multiple filter criteria before applying them all at once.
whenToUse: When you have multiple filter categories (status, priority, assignee, etc.)
When filter options are too numerous for inline filter chips
When users need to see all available filter options at once
In combination with a Filter button in the filter bar

components.name: GoabDivider
components.purpose: Separate Clear All from filter categories

pendingFilters.example: { status: string[], priority: string[], assignee: string[] }

appliedFilters.example: { status: string[], priority: string[], assignee: string[] }

example.description: Complete filter drawer with status, priority, and assignee categories

patternWorkflow.step1: User clicks 'Filter' button in filter bar
patternWorkflow.step2: Drawer opens with pendingFilters initialized from appliedFilters
patternWorkflow.step4: User clicks 'Apply filters' to commit or 'Cancel' to discard
patternWorkflow.step5: Applied filters shown as chips in filter bar with option to remove individually

variants.name: With search within categories
variants.description: Add GoabInput at top of long checkbox lists for searching options

variants.name: With date range filter
variants.description: Include GoabDatePicker for filtering by date ranges

variants.name: Collapsible categories
variants.description: Use GoabAccordion to collapse rarely-used filter categories

considerations: Use pending/applied pattern to allow users to make multiple selections before committing
When opening drawer, copy appliedFilters to pendingFilters so user starts where they left off
Extract filter options dynamically from data to ensure options match available values
Consider alphabetical sorting of options for predictability
Show 'Clear all filters' only when there are selections to clear
